name: Cassolette of Langoustine
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare fish veloute: Make fish stock, form a roux with equal parts oil and butter, season with salt and pepper.
2. Shell the langoustine.
3. Rinse scallops in cold water to clean.
4. Heat oil and butter in a sauté pan over medium heat.
5. Add shallots and sauté until softened.
6. Add langoustine, salt, and pepper; cook for about 1 minute per side.
7. Remove langoustine with a slotted spoon and set aside to drain.
8. In the same pan, add scallops and cook quickly until just opaque; remove with a slotted spoon and drain.
9. Deglaze the pan with lemon juice and vermouth, scraping up any browned bits.
10. Stir in cream and fish veloute, seasoning with salt and pepper. Simmer over low heat until sauce thickens, whisking occasionally.
11. Add julienne vegetables (carrot, leek, celery) and langoustine and scallops. Cook for a brief time until heated through.
12. Serve with individual portions: place three langoustine on each plate, add one scallop, pour sauce over the top, and garnish with chopped parsley.
